title: potentilla sterilis var. micrantha
intro: Potentilla sterilis var. micrantha, also known as dwarf cinquefoil, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Potentilla sterilis var. micrantha are numerous, including its potential to protect against cardiovascular disease, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, are often used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
